kids.question: Who wrote 2 Thessalonians?
kids.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:1 — "Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians."

kids.question: What did Paul say was growing in the Thessalonians?
Who will Jesus punish when He comes back?
kids.option_a: Those who do not obey the gospel
kids.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:8 — "taking vengeance on them that know not God, and that obey not the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ."

teens.question: What two things does Paul thank God for regarding the Thessalonians?
teens.option_a: Their growing faith and abounding love toward each other
teens.option_c: Their building program and membership growth
teens.option_d: Their obedience to Jewish law and temple attendance
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:3 — "your faith groweth exceedingly, and the charity of every one of you all toward each other aboundeth."

teens.question: What does Paul say is evidence that God's judgment is righteous?
teens.option_a: The Thessalonians' patient endurance of persecutions and tribulations shows they are counted worthy of the kingdom
teens.option_b: Their financial prosperity proves God's favor
teens.option_c: Their large church proves they are righteous
teens.option_d: Their lack of problems proves God's blessing
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:4-5 — "your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations… a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God, that ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom."

teens.question: How will Jesus be revealed from heaven?
teens.option_a: With His mighty angels in flaming fire
teens.option_b: On a white horse with a sword
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:7 — "the Lord Jesus shall be revealed from heaven with his mighty angels, In flaming fire."

teens.question: What punishment awaits those who do not obey the gospel?
teens.option_a: Everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord and the glory of His power
teens.option_d: Being sent to a different planet
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:9 — "Who shall be punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord, and from the glory of his power."

teens.question: What does Paul pray will be glorified in the Thessalonians?
teens.option_a: The name of our Lord Jesus Christ, and they in Him
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:12 — "That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and ye in him."

adults.question: What does Paul say is a righteous thing with God?
adults.option_a: To recompense tribulation to them that trouble the Thessalonians, and to give rest to the troubled
adults.option_b: To bless all people equally regardless of their actions
adults.option_c: To withhold judgment indefinitely
adults.option_d: To allow the wicked to prosper without consequence
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:6-7 — "it is a righteous thing with God to recompense tribulation to them that trouble you; And to you who are troubled rest with us."

adults.question: What two groups does Christ take vengeance on at His coming?
adults.option_a: Those who know not God and those who obey not the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ
adults.option_b: Only the Jews who rejected Him and the Romans who crucified Him
adults.option_c: Only false teachers and their followers
adults.option_d: Only demons and fallen angels
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:8 — "In flaming fire taking vengeance on them that know not God, and that obey not the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ."

adults.question: When does Paul say Christ will be admired?
adults.option_a: When He comes to be glorified in His saints and admired in all them that believe in that day
adults.option_b: When He establishes an earthly kingdom
adults.option_c: When He appears to the Roman emperor
adults.option_d: When the temple is rebuilt
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:10 — "When he shall come to be glorified in his saints, and to be admired in all them that believe… in that day."

adults.question: What does Paul pray God would count them worthy of?
adults.option_a: Their calling, and fulfil all the good pleasure of His goodness and the work of faith with power
adults.option_b: Their sacrifices and offerings
adults.option_c: Their leadership position in the church
adults.option_d: Their Hebrew ancestry
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:11 — "that our God would count you worthy of this calling, and fulfil all the good pleasure of his goodness, and the work of faith with power."

adults.question: What is the basis for Paul's prayer for the Thessalonians?
adults.option_a: According to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ
adults.option_b: According to their works and merit
adults.option_c: According to apostolic authority
adults.option_d: According to prophecy and signs
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:12 — "according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ."

adults.question: How does Paul describe the Thessalonians' persecutions?
adults.option_a: They endure them patiently, and Paul boasts about them in the churches of God
adults.option_b: They fled from them to other cities
adults.option_c: They fought back against their persecutors
adults.option_d: They complained bitterly to Paul
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:4 — "we ourselves glory in you in the churches of God for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that ye endure."

adults.question: What does "everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord" imply theologically?
adults.option_a: Eternal separation from God's beneficial presence — the punishment is both the loss of God's presence and the experience of His wrath
adults.option_b: Annihilation of the soul
adults.option_c: Temporary punishment followed by reconciliation
adults.option_d: Physical destruction of the body only
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:9 — "punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord, and from the glory of his power."

adults.question: What does Paul's statement "our testimony among you was believed" in verse 10 connect to?
adults.option_a: It connects the Thessalonians' faith to their participation in Christ's future glory — they believed, so they will be among those in whom He is glorified
adults.option_b: It proves Paul was a reliable witness in court
adults.option_c: It refers to miracles Paul performed among them
adults.option_d: It means they accepted his letter as Scripture
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 1:10 — "because our testimony among you was believed."

kids.question: What did Paul tell the Thessalonians not to be?
kids.option_a: Shaken in mind or troubled, as if the day of Christ had already come
kids.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:2 — "That ye be not soon shaken in mind, or be troubled… as that the day of Christ is at hand."

kids.question: Who is the "man of sin" who will try to deceive people?
kids.option_a: A wicked person who opposes God and sits in God's temple claiming to be God
kids.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:3-4 — "that man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition; Who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God."

kids.question: What did Paul tell believers to stand fast and hold?
kids.option_a: The traditions (teachings) they had been taught
kids.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:15 — "stand fast, and hold the traditions which ye have been taught."

teens.question: What must happen before the day of Christ comes?
teens.option_a: A falling away (apostasy) must come first and the man of sin must be revealed
teens.option_c: The temple must be rebuilt in Jerusalem
teens.option_d: Every person on earth must hear the gospel
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:3 — "that day shall not come, except there come a falling away first, and that man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition."

teens.question: What will the Lord Jesus do to the wicked one?
teens.option_a: Consume him with the spirit of His mouth and destroy him with the brightness of His coming
teens.option_b: Imprison him for a thousand years
teens.option_d: Send him to a distant land
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:8 — "whom the Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth, and shall destroy with the brightness of his coming."

teens.question: What deception will come upon those who refuse to love the truth?
teens.option_a: God shall send them strong delusion, that they should believe a lie
teens.option_d: They will forget everything they know
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:11 — "God shall send them strong delusion, that they should believe a lie."

teens.question: What is currently restraining the man of sin?
teens.option_a: A restrainer ("he who now letteth") who will be taken out of the way first
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:7 — "he who now letteth will let, until he be taken out of the way."

teens.question: What did God choose the Thessalonians to through from the beginning?
teens.option_a: Salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:13 — "God hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth."

adults.question: What does the man of sin do in the temple of God?
adults.option_a: He sits in the temple of God, showing himself that he is God
adults.option_b: He offers sacrifices to pagan idols
adults.option_c: He destroys the temple completely
adults.option_d: He locks the doors and keeps everyone out
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:4 — "he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God."

adults.question: What accompanies the working of Satan through the lawless one?
adults.option_a: All power, signs, and lying wonders, and all deceivableness of unrighteousness
adults.option_b: True miracles that cannot be distinguished from God's
adults.option_c: Political manipulation and military conquest only
adults.option_d: Scholarly arguments and philosophical reasoning
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:9-10 — "whose coming is after the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders, And with all deceivableness of unrighteousness."

adults.question: Why does God send strong delusion to the unbelievers?
adults.option_a: Because they received not the love of the truth that they might be saved — it is judicial judgment on willful rejection
adults.option_b: Because God wants them to perish
adults.option_c: Because they never had the chance to hear the truth
adults.option_d: Because they were predestined to be lost
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:10-11 — "because they received not the love of the truth, that they might be saved. And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion."

adults.question: What does "the mystery of iniquity doth already work" mean?
adults.option_a: The spirit and principle of lawlessness was already secretly active in Paul's day, awaiting full manifestation
adults.option_b: A specific crime was being committed in the church
adults.option_c: The Roman Empire was declining
adults.option_d: Jewish false teachers were already in Thessalonica
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:7 — "the mystery of iniquity doth already work: only he who now letteth will let, until he be taken out of the way."

adults.question: What two means of teaching does Paul reference in verse 15?
adults.option_a: By word (oral teaching) and by epistle (written letter) — both are authoritative apostolic tradition
adults.option_b: By parables and by allegories
adults.option_c: By miracles and by visions
adults.option_d: By fasting and by prayer
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:15 — "hold the traditions which ye have been taught, whether by word, or our epistle."

adults.question: What does Paul pray God would do for the Thessalonians' hearts?
adults.option_a: Comfort their hearts and stablish them in every good word and work
adults.option_b: Reveal to them the date of Christ's return
adults.option_c: Remove all their earthly troubles
adults.option_d: Give them positions of authority
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:16-17 — "comfort your hearts, and stablish you in every good word and work."

adults.question: What is the ultimate fate of all who did not believe the truth?
adults.option_a: That they all might be damned who believed not the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness
adults.option_b: They will have a second chance after death
adults.option_c: They will be given more time to repent
adults.option_d: They will simply cease to exist
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:12 — "That they all might be damned who believed not the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness."

adults.question: How does Paul describe God's love toward the Thessalonians?
adults.option_a: God loved them and gave them everlasting consolation and good hope through grace
adults.option_b: God tested them severely to prove their faith
adults.option_c: God remained distant until they proved themselves
adults.option_d: God offered conditional love based on their obedience
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 2:16 — "God, even our Father, which hath loved us, and hath given us everlasting consolation and good hope through grace."

kids.question: What does Paul say people should do instead of being idle?
kids.option_a: Work quietly and earn their own bread
kids.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:12 — "that with quietness they work, and eat their own bread."

kids.question: What should we not be weary of doing?
kids.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:13 — "be not weary in well doing."

kids.question: What did Paul ask the Thessalonians to pray for?
kids.option_a: That the word of the Lord would spread quickly
kids.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:1 — "pray for us, that the word of the Lord may have free course, and be glorified."

teens.question: What rule did Paul give about those who refuse to work?
teens.option_a: If any would not work, neither should he eat
teens.option_b: Give them extra food to encourage them
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:10 — "if any would not work, neither should he eat."

teens.question: What did Paul say about disorderly brethren?
teens.option_a: Withdraw from them, but do not count them as enemies — admonish them as brothers
teens.option_b: Expel them permanently from the church
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:6,14-15 — "withdraw yourselves from every brother that walketh disorderly… yet count him not as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother."

teens.question: How did Paul set an example for the Thessalonians?
teens.option_a: He worked night and day so as not to be a burden to any of them
teens.option_d: He gave away all his money
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:8 — "Neither did we eat any man's bread for nought; but wrought with labour and travail night and day, that we might not be chargeable to any of you."

teens.question: What does Paul say the Lord is faithful to do?
teens.option_a: Stablish (establish) them and keep them from evil
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:3 — "the Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep you from evil."

teens.question: How does Paul describe the busybodies in the church?
teens.option_a: They work not at all but are busybodies — meddling in others' affairs instead of working
teens.option_b: They are hard workers who take on too much
teens.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:11 — "we hear that there are some which walk among you disorderly, working not at all, but are busybodies."

adults.question: What does Paul pray for regarding unreasonable and wicked men?
adults.option_a: That he and his companions may be delivered from them, for all men have not faith
adults.option_b: That they be struck down by God
adults.option_c: That they be converted immediately
adults.option_d: That they be imprisoned by the Romans
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:2 — "that we may be delivered from unreasonable and wicked men: for all men have not faith."

adults.question: What does Paul say was the purpose of his example of working?
adults.option_a: To make himself an ensample (example) for them to follow, not because he had no right to support
adults.option_b: Because he could not find donors
adults.option_c: Because manual labor was required by Jewish law
adults.option_d: Because the Thessalonians refused to support him
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:9 — "Not because we have not power, but to make ourselves an ensample unto you to follow us."

adults.question: What connection exists between idleness and the eschatological confusion in Thessalonica?
adults.option_a: Some Thessalonians, believing Christ's return was imminent, stopped working and became idle busybodies — Paul corrects both their theology and behavior
adults.option_b: The idle were pagans who hadn't yet converted
adults.option_c: Idleness was a cultural norm in Thessalonica
adults.option_d: The idle were wealthy patrons who didn't need to work
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:10-12 connects the command to work with the correction of eschatological excitement leading to abandonment of daily responsibilities.

adults.question: What disciplinary measure does Paul prescribe for those who disobey this epistle?
adults.option_a: Note that man and have no company with him, that he may be ashamed — yet admonish him as a brother, not an enemy
adults.option_b: Excommunicate him permanently
adults.option_c: Fine him and take his property
adults.option_d: Report him to the civil authorities
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:14-15 — "note that man, and have no company with him, that he may be ashamed. Yet count him not as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother."

adults.question: What does Paul direct the Lord to do for their hearts?
adults.option_a: "The Lord direct your hearts into the love of God, and into the patient waiting for Christ"
adults.option_b: "The Lord fill your hearts with knowledge"
adults.option_c: "The Lord remove all fear from your hearts"
adults.option_d: "The Lord grant your hearts' desires"
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:5 — "the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God, and into the patient waiting for Christ."

adults.question: What does Paul say about the tradition (teaching) they received from him?
adults.option_a: They should withdraw from every brother who walks disorderly and not after the tradition received from the apostles
adults.option_b: Traditions are optional guidelines
adults.option_c: Only written traditions are authoritative
adults.option_d: Traditions should be modified by each generation
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:6 — "withdraw yourselves from every brother that walketh disorderly, and not after the tradition which he received of us."

adults.question: How does Paul authenticate this letter?
adults.option_a: He writes the salutation with his own hand, which is the token (sign) in every epistle
adults.option_b: He seals it with his apostolic ring
adults.option_c: He has two witnesses sign it
adults.option_d: He sends it via a trusted courier only
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:17 — "The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I write."

adults.question: What comprehensive peace blessing does Paul give?
adults.option_a: "Now the Lord of peace himself give you peace always by all means"
adults.option_b: "May the God of war protect you from your enemies"
adults.option_c: "May the Lord give you financial peace"
adults.option_d: "Peace be upon Israel and the church"
adults.explanation: 2 Thessalonians 3:16 — "Now the Lord of peace himself give you peace always by all means. The Lord be with you all."
